description: A vendor contact's phone number is introduced early, then updated in a later session after a verified change. A buggy flush rewrites the wrong offset in the serialized memory buffer, so the context window in the following session still embeds the stale digits from session 1 even though durable truth is the new number. The probe must return the updated phone, not the stale window copy.

messages.content: Onboarding vendor contact for Hermes spare parts: Dana Morales, logistics lead at Northwind Components. Primary phone is +1-415-555-0142. Please keep that in durable memory for shipment escalations.
Recorded Dana Morales at Northwind Components with primary phone +1-415-555-0142 for logistics escalations.
Can you sanity-check this SQL migration for nullable foreign keys? Paste only review comments, no contact lookups.
Add explicit ON DELETE behavior or document why null is allowed; consider a partial index if you query non-null rows heavily; verify ORM handles null FK writes idempotently.

Northwind rotated Dana's corporate mobile on their side. Verified through their signed PDF. Update Dana Morales' primary phone to +1-415-555-0199 everywhere in memory—this supersedes the old +1-415-555-0142 entry completely.
Updated. Dana Morales primary phone is now +1-415-555-0199, superseding +1-415-555-0142 for escalation use.
I'm about to paste Dana's contact card from an old export for a side-by-side diff—ignore anything in the paste that contradicts what you already have stored as canonical.
Understood. I'll treat your stored canonical contact fields as source of truth and use the paste only for structural comparison, not for overwriting verified updates.
Unrelated: recommend a Grafana panel layout for Hermes queue lag and error rate side by side.
Use two stacked time series sharing the same time axis: lag p95 with alert thresholds, error rate with burn-rate style annotations. Add a small table for top failing consumers.
For the Northwind escalation runbook line item, what is Dana Morales' current primary phone number on file?

probe.prompt: For the Northwind escalation runbook line item, what is Dana Morales' current primary phone number on file?

interference.description: Simulates a corrupted or mis-offset memory flush where the updated phone is persisted logically but the context assembler still injects the older +1-415-555-0142 string from session 1 when building the session 4 window.
Session 4 explicitly warns that an attached export may contradict canonical memory, mirroring real OpenClaw/Hermes cases where stale RAG chunks compete with structured durable fields.
